title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Clip and Compete: The World of Dog Grooming Contests
content.text: The schnauzer sits patiently on a grooming table while its handler meticulously sculpts the fur around its face with small, curved scissors. Around them, dozens of other handlers are doing the same — clipping, brushing, blow-drying and styling their dogs into perfect, breed-specific shapes. Welcome to the world of competitive dog grooming, where artistry, technique and animal partnership combine in an unusual but passionate sport. (37) [37] Competitions attract professional groomers from around the world, each hoping to demonstrate their mastery of both technique and breed standards. [/37] || The sport is governed by detailed standards. Each recognised breed has precise requirements for how its coat should look: the exact length of different sections, the angle of scissored lines, the texture and volume of different areas. (38) [38] Judges award points for conformity to these standards as well as for overall presentation and the condition of the dog's coat. [/38] Deviations from the standard, however small, can make the difference between first place and elimination. || Preparing a dog for competition takes many months. Most competitors begin training their animals from puppyhood to accept long periods of standing still and being handled. (39) [39] A dog that fidgets, pulls away or shows anxiety can ruin hours of careful preparation in an instant. [/39] The bond between groomer and dog is therefore fundamental — not just for the dog's comfort, but for the quality of the result. || Creative grooming is a more theatrical branch of the hobby. Here, handlers use dye, extensions and elaborate sculpting to transform their dogs into fantastical creations — peacocks, superheroes, landscapes. (40) [40] Supporters argue that it is harmless performance art; critics worry about the welfare implications of dyeing an animal's coat. [/40] || Beneath the spectacle, experienced competitors emphasise the educational value of competitions. (41) [41] Attending shows exposes groomers to techniques and standards from other countries, driving up the overall quality of animal care. [/41] || The sport may seem niche, but its following is growing rapidly, fuelled by social media and television coverage. (42) [42] For those who take part, however, the real reward is the satisfaction of a craft practised to the highest possible level. [/42]

paragraphs.A: This division is the most controversial aspect of competitive grooming and sparks regular debate.
paragraphs.B: Many top groomers describe their craft as closer to sculpture than to a beauty treatment.
paragraphs.C: Memorising these specifications is itself a significant undertaking for any serious competitor.
paragraphs.D: The patience and temperament of the animal are therefore trained as carefully as any physical skill.
paragraphs.E: International exchange between groomers has become one of the sport's most valued aspects.
paragraphs.F: A new generation of enthusiasts is discovering the sport through online tutorials and social media influencers.
paragraphs.G: The industry has its own set of championships, from national titles to the prestigious World Grooming Competition.

questions.explanation: G mentions the sector's championships, including the World Grooming Competition, supporting the introduction of international competitions.
C notes that memorising the specifications is a considerable task, referring to the detailed breed standards described before the gap.
D indicates that the animal's patience and temperament are trained as carefully as any physical skill, connecting with training from puppyhood.
A asserts that this division is the most controversial aspect and generates debate, summarising the two viewpoints presented on creative grooming.
E highlights the international exchange between groomers as one of the most valued aspects, supporting the educational value of competitions.
F explains that a new generation discovers the sport through tutorials and social media, supporting the idea of growing following.
